callback.name: PreUpdate
callback.summary: Called at the start of every frame.

callback.name: Update
callback.summary: Called at the middle of every frame.

callback.name: PostUpdate
callback.summary: Called at the end of every frame.

callback.name: OnEnable
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour gets enabled.

callback.name: OnDisable
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour gets disabled.

callback.name: Awake
callback.summary: Called when the behaviour is created.

callback.name: OnDestroy
callback.summary: Called when the behavior is going to be destroyed.

callback.name: Start
callback.summary: Called before the first enabled frame ever.

callback.name: FixedUpdate
callback.summary: Called every frame that physics updates.

callback.name: OnMouseDown
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a collider and the mouse is just clicked inside it.

callback.name: OnMouseDownUp
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a collider and the mouse was down and now is just released inside it.

callback.name: OnMouseDrag
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a collider and the mouse is dragging it.

callback.name: OnMouseEnter
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a collider and the mouse pointer enters the collider.

callback.name: OnMouseExit
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a collider and the mouse pointer leaves the collider.

callback.name: OnMouseHover
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a collider and the mouse is inside it.

callback.name: OnMouseUp
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a collider and the mouse is just released inside it.

callback.name: OnBecameVisible
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a renderer and it has just become visible for any camera.

callback.name: OnBecameInvisible
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a renderer and it has just become invisible for every camera.

callback.name: OnCollisionEnter
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a Collider or a RigidBody and it has just started colliding to another.
callback.param: Handles all the information about the collision.

callback.name: OnCollisionStay
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a Collider or a RigidBody and it is colliding to another.
callback.param: Handles all the information about the collision.

callback.name: OnCollisionExit
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a Collider or a RigidBody and it has just stopped colliding to another.
callback.param: Handles all the information about the collision.

callback.name: OnTriggerEnter
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a Collider trigger or a RigidBody and it just entered another inside.
callback.param: The other collider that has entered inside the trigger.

callback.name: OnTriggerStay
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a Collider trigger or a RigidBody and it is another inside.
callback.param: The other collider that is inside the trigger.

callback.name: OnTriggerExit
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a Collider trigger or a RigidBody and it has just left another.
callback.param: The other collider that has just left the trigger.

callback.name: OnJointBreak
callback.summary: Called when this behaviour owns a Joint and it breaks.
callback.param: The joint object that has just been broken.

callback.name: OnDrawGizmos
callback.summary: Allows drawing custom gizmos to the scene view. This callback is only called in editor mode.

callback.name: OnDrawGizmosSelected
callback.summary: Allows drawing custom gizmos to the scene view only when the Entity is selected. This callback is only called in editor mode.

callback.name: OnEdited
callback.summary: Called when the user modifies a property with the Inspector or when the behaviour is loaded. This callback is only called in editor mode.

callback.name: Reset
callback.summary: Called when the user clicks the Reset button in the Inspector's context menu or when the behaviour is added for the first time. This callback is only called in editor mode.

callback.name: Reset
callback.summary: Called when the user clicks the Reset button in the Inspector or when the Object is created for the first time. This callback is only called in editor mode.

callback.name: OnEdited
callback.summary: Called when the user modifies a property with the Inspector. This callback is only called in editor mode.

callback.name: OnEnable
callback.summary: Called when this object gets enabled.

callback.name: OnDisable
callback.summary: Called when this object gets disabled.

callback.name: Awake
callback.summary: Called when the behaviour is created.

callback.name: Reset
callback.summary: Called when the user clicks the Reset button in the Inspector's context menu or when the behaviour is added for the first time. This callback is only called in editor mode.

callback.name: OnDestroy
callback.summary: Called when the behaviour is going to be destroyed.

callback.name: OnStateEnter
callback.summary: Called when the state machine enters to this state.
callback.param: The animator object where this state is active.
Holds information about the current state.

callback.name: OnStateUpdate
callback.summary: Called every frame this state is active.
callback.param: The animator object where this state is active.
Holds information about the current state.

callback.name: OnStateExit
callback.summary: Called when this state stops being the active one.
callback.param: The animator object where this state is active.
Holds information about the current state.

callback.name: OnGraphUpdaterStart
callback.summary: Called when a graph updater starts updating this graph node.
callback.param: The graph updater that is starting to update the graph node.

callback.name: OnGraphUpdaterEnd
callback.summary: Called when a graph updater stops updating the graph node, informing whether it has finished or not.
callback.param: The graph updater that has stopped updating the graph node.
Whether it has finished (true) or has been stopped (false).

callback.name: Init
callback.summary: Called once when this editor behaviour instance is initialized by the editor.

callback.name: OnDestroy
callback.summary: Called once when this editor behaviour instance is destroyed by the editor.

callback.name: Update
callback.summary: Called every editor frame.

callback.name: OnCustomInspector
callback.summary: Called when the inspector of the behaviour requested with the attribute CustomInspector needs to be drawn. This allows to create a custom inspector for user script behaviours.
callback.param: The target object of the inspector.

callback.name: OnBecameInvisible
callback.summary: Called when the window is hidden.

callback.name: OnBecameVisible
callback.summary: Called when the window is shown.

callback.name: Awake
callback.summary: Called when the window is created.

callback.name: OnFocused
callback.summary: Called when the window gains focus.

callback.name: OnLostFocus
callback.summary: Called when the window loses focus.

callback.name: OnDestroy
callback.summary: Called when the window is about to be destroyed.

callback.name: OnGUI
callback.summary: Called to render the window's UI using the CometEditor::GUI API.

callback.name: OnSaveChanges
callback.summary: Called when the window is requested to save its changes.

callback.name: OnDiscardChanges
callback.summary: Called when the window is requested to discard its changes.

callback.name: OnAbortCloseSaveChanges
callback.summary: Called when the popup to confirm save changes is aborted with the 'X' button.

callback.name: OnGetWindowConfig
callback.summary: Called when the window is going to be created to get its configuration. This allows to configure the window properties like its size, whether it is resizable or not, etc.
callback.return: Returns the configuration to use for the window.

attribute.name: AssetIcon
attribute.summary: Attribute that can be used with fields that are Sprite, Sprite[], Texture2D or Texture2D[] if they belong to a CometObject. If so, it will set the given Sprite/Texture2D as the icon of the asset inside the Panel Project.

attribute.name: AssetMenu
attribute.summary: Adds a new entry to the Assets/Create submenu with the given data.
attribute.param: The name of the new file to create.
The name of the menu item.

attribute.name: ContextMenu
attribute.summary: Adds the method to the context menu of the behaviour when right-clicking the behaviour header in the Inspector.
attribute.param: The name of the context menu item.

attribute.name: MainMenuItem
attribute.summary: Adds the method to the main menu bar with the given path. The path is defined with slashes separating the different levels of the menu. Example: "Tools/Custom/Do Something" will create a "Do Something" item inside a "Custom" submenu of the "Tools" menu.
attribute.param: The path of the main menu item.

attribute.name: MainMenu
attribute.summary: Adds the method to the main menu bar as a submenu, it will be called when the submenu is opened in order to customize it. The path is defined with slashes separating the different levels of the menu. Example: "Tools/Custom" will create "Custom" submenu inside the "Tools" menu.
attribute.param: The path of the main menu.

attribute.name: MainMenuItemWindow
attribute.summary: Adds an option to open an EditorWindow from the main menu bar with the given path. The path is defined with slashes separating the different levels of the menu. Example: "Tools/Custom/Do Something" will create a "Do Something" item inside a "Custom" submenu of the "Tools" menu. This attribute is only valid for scripts that inherit from CometEditor::EditorWindow.
attribute.param: The path of the main menu item window.
The title of the main menu item window. If this parameter is empty, it will use the name of the class as the window title.

attribute.name: ContextMenuItem
attribute.summary: Adds a method to the context menu of the field for the behaviour when right-clicking the field in the Inspector.
attribute.param: The name of the context menu item.
The name of the method to call.

attribute.name: Header
attribute.summary: Creates a header above the field in the Inspector with the given name.
attribute.param: The name of the header.

attribute.name: HelpURL
attribute.summary: Adds a help icon to the top right of the Inspector that opens the given URL.
attribute.param: The URL to open when clicking the help icon.

attribute.name: GraphNodeMenuItem
attribute.summary: Registers this node in the node creation popup with a specified location and allowed graph node classes. Only applies to NodeGraph classes.
attribute.param: The path where the node appears in the creation popup.
Comma-separated list of NodeGraph class names where this node can be created. If empty, the node is valid for all NodeGraph classes.

attribute.name: NodeColor
attribute.summary: Sets the color of the node in the graph editor. Only applies to GraphNode classes.
attribute.param: The red component of the node color.
The green component of the node color.
The blue component of the node color.

attribute.name: HideInInspector
attribute.summary: Hides the field or method from being shown in the Inspector.

attribute.name: PreviewTexture
attribute.summary: Shows a preview of the texture or sprite in the inspector if the field is of type Texture2D, Texture2D[], Sprite or Sprite[].
attribute.param: The size of the preview texture. If 0, it will be auto calculated.

attribute.name: RequiereBehaviour
attribute.summary: Indicates that the behaviour requires the given behaviour to be attached to the same Entity. If the required behaviour is not attached, it will be automatically added.
attribute.param: The name of the required behaviour. Example: "CometEngine::Camera".

attribute.name: CustomInspector
attribute.summary: This attribute can only be used for classes that derive from CometEditor::EditorBehaviour to override the inspector of classes that derive from CometEngine::CometBehaviour. It is used to create custom inspectors for user script behaviours.
attribute.param: The name of the user script behaviour to override its inspector. Example: "MyNamespace::MyBehaviour".

attribute.name: SelectionBase
attribute.summary: Indicates that the Entity that owns this behaviour should be selected when clicking any of its children in the Scene view.

attribute.name: Serialize
attribute.summary: Indicates that the field or method should be serialized and shown in the Inspector.

attribute.name: ShowButton
attribute.summary: Shows a button in the Inspector that calls the method when clicked.
attribute.param: The name of the button.

attribute.name: ShowSlider
attribute.summary: Shows a slider in the Inspector to modify the value of the field. The field must be a numeric type.
attribute.param: The minimum value of the slider.
The maximum value of the slider.

attribute.name: Spacing
attribute.summary: Adds spacing in the Inspector before the field.
attribute.param: The amount of extra spacing to add before the field.

attribute.name: TextArea
attribute.summary: Shows a multi-line text area in the Inspector to edit the value of the field. The field must be of type String.
attribute.param: The number of lines to show. If 0, it will be auto calculated.

attribute.name: Tooltip
attribute.summary: Shows a tooltip when hovering the field or method in the Inspector with the mouse.
attribute.param: The text to show in the tooltip.

attribute.name: Tab
attribute.summary: The field will now be showed inside a tab with all the other fields that request the same tab consecutive.
attribute.param: The name of the tab.
If it is true, all consecutive tab will appear on the same tab bar. If it is set to false, a new tab bar will be created.

attribute.name: TreeNode
attribute.summary: The field will now be showed inside a tree node with all the other fields that request the same tree node name.
attribute.param: The name of the node.

attribute.name: TreeNodeDefaultOpen
attribute.summary: If the field is a class or an array, it will be shown as a tree node in the Inspector. This attribute indicates that the tree node should be open by default.

attribute.name: UniqueAnimatorStateBehaviour
attribute.summary: Indicates that only one instance of this AnimatorStateBehaviour will exist so all animator controllers will share the same instance.
